title: Fluorescently labeled polyamine uptake (via confocal microscopy)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5k1ne","text":"This method involves visualizing polyamine uptake in cells using fluorescently labeled polyamines and confocal microscopy. Cells are incubated with fluorescently tagged polyamines to allow uptake, followed by washing to remove extracellular signal. Confocal microscopy is then used to detect and quantify intracellular fluorescence, enabling the study of polyamine transport dynamics, subcellular localization, and the effects of specific treatments or genetic modifications on polyamine uptake.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Seed cells 96-well plate. Allow the cells to attach and grow for approximately 24:00:00 .
The day of the experiment, remove cell culture medium and add fluorescently labeled polyamines to the cells in a final volume of 1000 µL, / well .
Incubate at 37 °C with 5% CO₂ for the desired duration, ensuring protection from light.
Wash cells 3 times in PBS-T ( 00:05:00 each wash) at Room temperature on a rocker.
Incubate cells with DAPI (1:5000 in PBS-T) for 00:15:00 at Room temperature , protected from light, on a rocker.
Wash cells 3 times in PBS-T ( 00:05:00 each wash) on a rocker, protected from light.
Wash cells Overnight with PBS-T at 4 °C on a rocker, protected from light.
Proceed with imaging or additional staining.
Fix cells with 4% PFA in PBS for 00:30:00 at 37 °C .
creator: Elena Coccia
